meta.title: A3 — Constraint Engineering Session Recap
meta.description: Full bucketing: 224 skills × 6 frameworks across 5-phase SDLC loop

props.title: Constraint Engineering: The Full Picture
props.subtitle: 224 skills bucketed across 6 frameworks, 5-phase SDLC loop, and the skill graph concept

props.content: A constraint is a flowmind definition. A harness is the tooling to run it. **Leviathan is a meta-harness** — it defines and runs its own constraint harnesses. This session mapped every surface, every framework, and every gap. What follows is the complete picture.

props.label: 5-PHASE COMPARISON
props.title: The Universal SDLC Loop: Lev vs OOO vs OMX
props.subtitle: Every framework converges on the same 5-phase cycle. Lev's primitives are the most composable.

props.content: Lev dominates Phase 2 (Plan: 182/mo) and Phase 4 (Evaluate: constraint engineering is Lev-only). OOO dominates Phase 1 (mathematical readiness gate) and Phase 5 (structured convergence). OMX dominates Phase 3 (tmux team operator UX). The 1.0 play: combine all three.

props.label: PRODUCT VISION
props.title: The Skill Graph: Graph-as-Search-Query
props.subtitle: Put everything on the graph. The request shape IS the search query.

props.content:
Today: user says '/work' and a single skill fires. Tomorrow: user submits a **proposal** — a graph fragment with 5 nodes (goal, constraints, entities, prior-art, lifecycle stage). That graph fragment becomes a **search query** against the full skill graph (224 skills + their connections). The system returns: "For this proposal shape, invoke /interview (ambiguity > 0.3), then /prior-art (no existing research), then /arch (architecture impact detected), then /work (execute with lev-ralph flow)."

The graph overlay replaces manual skill selection with **structural matching**. A proposal that looks like a planning task (nodes: goal + constraints + no code) matches the planning cluster. A proposal that looks like a debugging task (nodes: error + stacktrace + affected files) matches the debugging cluster. The shape IS the query.

props.title: Example: Graph Overlay Search
props.content:
**Input**: Proposal with nodes {goal: 'add browser MCP tools', constraints: 'must pass adversarial review', entities: 'plugins/browser-cascade', prior_art: 'none found', lifecycle: 'planning'}

**Graph match**: Planning cluster (ambiguity=0.4 → needs interview) + Browser domain (ConstraintDriver: BrowserConstraintDriver) + No prior art (triggers /prior-art + /lev-research)

**Output**: /interview → /prior-art → /arch (browser cascade architecture) → /work (plan phase with deepen-plan.flow) → lev-ralph (execute) → adversarial-review (verify)

The proposal shape determined the entire workflow. No manual skill selection.

props.label: GAP ANALYSIS
props.title: What Lev is Missing (from the 6 frameworks)
props.subtitle: 5 concrete gaps to close

props.title: Mathematical Readiness Gate
props.content:
**From**: OOO (ambiguity scoring) + OMX ($deep-interview)
**What**: Numeric clarity metric gating spec crystallization. Formula: ambiguity = 1 - sum(clarity_i x weight_i), gate at 0.2.
**Lev has**: DNA constraints validate code, not requirements prose.
**Fix**: Bake into /work interview phase. Algo-driven depth selection.

props.title: HUD / Status Overlay
props.content:
**From**: OMX (omx hud --json) + Hermes (hermes status) + OpenClaw (doctor)
**What**: Real-time execution status visible to operator.
**Lev has**: Nothing comparable.
**Fix**: A2's P2 deliverable — tmux floating pane dashboard.

props.title: Machine API for Tasks
props.content:
**From**: OMX (create-task, claim-task, transition-task-status, write-worker-inbox)
**What**: Programmatic task lifecycle control.
**Lev has**: bd (beads) CLI but no HTTP/WS machine API.
**Fix**: Expose bd operations as MCP tools or HTTP endpoints.

props.title: Composite Drift Score
props.content:
**From**: OOO (goal 50% + constraint 30% + ontology 20%)
**What**: Weighted drift metric combining multiple dimensions.
**Lev has**: drift_threshold=3 (count, not weighted composite).
**Fix**: Add weighted composite to plugins/validator.

props.title: Stagnation / Oscillation Detection
props.content:
**From**: OOO (period-2 oscillation, 70% question overlap, 30-gen cap)
**What**: Loop pathology guards preventing stuck or cycling iterations.
**Lev has**: circuit_breaker=3 (simple count).
**Fix**: Add oscillation detection to FlowMind loop runtime.

props.label: PRODUCT DESIGN
props.title: Crystallization: What Gets Built
props.subtitle: The convergence of 224 skills, 6 frameworks, and one graph

props.title: /work Gets Smarter
props.content:
**Today**: /work is a prose discipline router (182 uses/mo). /interview is separate (71 uses/mo). /prior-art is separate (11/mo).

**Tomorrow**: /work bakes in interview + prior-art. Depth is algo-driven by ambiguity/complexity score. Deep interview = alignment = OOO ambiguity scoring + prior-art + deep research = best planner in the ecosystem.

The arch-interview flow is the case study: token-efficient with warm workers, bidirectional steering. That's the magic — not the Socratic questions, but the unfolding.

props.title: Skills-DB: From 224 to 30 Top-Level
props.content:
**Sub-lev hub** for the full inventory. JSON/YAML/file-driven seed DB.

**Tier 1 (daily, 2)**: work, interview
**Tier 2 (weekly, 10)**: lev-intake, cdo, using-superpowers, skill-builder, codex-autoresearch, cass, arch, prior-art, agent-browser, now
**Tier 3 (essential, 8)**: tribunal, stack, handoff, sync, brainstorming, lev-plan, lev-research, defendr
**Tier 4 (specialist, ~30)**: testing, GEO, gitnexus, platform integrations
**Tier 5 (archive, ~170)**: available but not top-level

The skills-db makes all 224 discoverable via graph search. The 30 top-level are the optimized surface.

props.title: Stage-Grouped Cross-Framework Review
props.content:
Shared ontology/taxonomy enables: 'Show me everything at planning/alignment stage.'

This returns — across ALL frameworks:
- Lev: /interview, /arch, /writing-plans, deepen-plan.flow, spec-lifecycle.flow
- OOO: Socratic Interviewer, Seed crystallization, Double Diamond
- OMX: $deep-interview, $ralplan
- Hermes: setup wizard sections

All tagged with the same stage label, all comparable. The taxonomy IS the search index.

props.label: NEXT
props.title: Critical Path
props.subtitle: What happens in the next session

props.title: 1. Bake interview+prior-art into /work
props.content: Ambiguity scoring determines depth. /work becomes the single entry point.

props.title: 2. Build skills-db seed structure
props.content: JSON/YAML registry of 224 skills with category, tier, usage, graph connections.

props.title: 3. Fix 4 security P0s via lev-ralph
props.content: Shell injection, env leak, cipher, eval. ~2 hours. Unblocks npm publish.

props.title: 4. Prototype graph-as-search overlay
props.content: Proposal graph fragment → skill graph match → automated workflow selection.

props.title: 5. Build browser+MCP domain harness
props.content: First community/examples/ entry. Domain #1 of the constraint engineering rollout.
